parentQuestion: Positive feedback mechanisms tend to enhance the original stimulus so that the response is accelerated.

scaffolds.q: The body uses feedback mechanisms to control its internal processes.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Feedback loops allow the body to monitor conditions and respond appropriately.

scaffolds.q: There are TWO main types of feedback: negative and positive.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! They do opposite things - negative opposes changes, positive amplifies them.

scaffolds.q: NEGATIVE feedback OPPOSES changes - it brings things back toward normal.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Like a thermostat - too hot, cooling kicks in; too cold, heating kicks in.

scaffolds.q: POSITIVE feedback AMPLIFIES changes - it makes the response bigger.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The output feeds back to INCREASE the original stimulus.

scaffolds.q: Blood clotting uses positive feedback - each step triggers MORE clotting.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Clotting factors activate more clotting factors in an explosive cascade.

scaffolds.q: Childbirth contractions use positive feedback - contractions trigger stronger contractions.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Oxytocin causes contractions, which trigger more oxytocin, more contractions...

scaffolds.q: Positive feedback ENHANCES and ACCELERATES the original response.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Unlike negative feedback which opposes, positive feedback accelerates toward a goal.

scaffolds.q: Positive feedback mechanisms AMPLIFY the original stimulus.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The response enhances and accelerates the original change.

scaffolds.q: The statement is TRUE - positive feedback enhances and accelerates the response.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! This is the defining characteristic of positive feedback.
